name: La Costera y La Vall d'Albaida — Circular from Moixent
name_original: La Costera y La Vall d'Albaida — Circular desde Moixent
region_intro: Road cycling around Moixent offers a diverse and challenging terrain within the La Costera region of Valencia, Spain. The area is characterized by the Serra Grossa mountain range, providing significant elevation gains and panoramic views. Cyclists navigate through the Valley of Les Alcusses, a landscape featuring vineyards, olive groves, almond trees, and pine forests. This blend of mountainous and agricultural scenery creates varied routes for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Moixent The most popular road cycling route is La Costera y La Vall d'Albaida — Circular from Moixent, a 38.2 miles (61.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 1 minute to complete. This difficult route explores the scenic valleys and agricultural landscapes of the region. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Montesa Castle Loop from Moixent, a difficult 46.2 miles (74.4 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and passes by historical landmarks. Local road cyclists also love the Navalón and La Font de la Figuera Circular, a 30.6 miles (49.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 48 minutes. Road cycling around Moixent is defined by the Serra Grossa mountain range, extensive vineyards, and olive groves, with routes offering options for moderate to very difficult ability levels. The routes in Moixent are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from 3 reviews. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Moixent's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Moixent?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes around Moixent, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists. These routes are primarily categorized as moderate to difficult, with 5 moderate and 12 difficult options to choose from.

Q: What kind of terrain and difficulty can I expect on road cycling routes in Moixent?
A: Road cycling in Moixent is characterized by the Serra Grossa mountain range, offering significant elevation gains from 1,000 to over 3,000 feet. Routes feature well-paved surfaces, though some segments can be unpaved and more challenging. You'll encounter classic climbs like &lt;strong&gt;Puerto del Moro&lt;/strong&gt;, known for its good pavement and scenic views, making it suitable for cyclists with good to very good fitness.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Moixent?
A: Yes, Moixent offers several excellent circular road cycling routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e839885564/la-costera-y-la-vall-d-albaida-circular-from-moixent" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Costera y La Vall d'Albaida — Circular from Moixent&lt;/a&gt;, which is 38.2 miles (61.5 km) long. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e859954836/navalon-and-la-font-de-la-figuera-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navalón and La Font de la Figuera Circular&lt;/a&gt;, covering 30.6 miles (49.3 km) through varied terrain.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features I can see along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes around Moixent pass through diverse landscapes. You can explore the picturesque &lt;strong&gt;Valley of Les Alcusses&lt;/strong&gt; with its vineyards, olive groves, and almond trees. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5285811" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iberian Site of Bastida de les Alcusses&lt;/a&gt;, an important archaeological site, is also accessible. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3485518" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pou Clar Natural Pools&lt;/a&gt;, though closer to Bocairent, are within reach and offer a beautiful natural stop.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Moixent?
A: The road cycling routes in Moixent are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from 3 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging and diverse terrain, the stunning natural landscapes, and the well-paved roads that make for an enjoyable experience. Over 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the area's varied routes.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by historical sites?
A: Yes, several routes offer glimpses of historical sites. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e841303093/montesa-castle-loop-from-moixent" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montesa Castle Loop from Moixent&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 46.2-mile (74.4 km) path that passes by historical landmarks, including the ruins of the Muslim-era Moixent Castle. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5285811" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iberian Site of Bastida de les Alcusses&lt;/a&gt; is another significant historical point of interest in the region.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Moixent?
A: Moixent offers good conditions for road cycling for much of the year. The region's Mediterranean climate means pleasant temperatures for spring and autumn rides. While summer can be warm, early mornings or late afternoons are often suitable. The diverse terrain ensures that there are routes enjoyable across different seasons.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for experienced road cyclists looking for a challenge?
A: Absolutely. Moixent is known for its challenging routes with significant elevation gains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38242612" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pou Clar Natural Area – Banyeres loop from Moixent&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 70.3-mile (113.2 km) route with over 1,800 meters of elevation gain, providing a substantial test for experienced riders. Many routes feature climbs used in professional cycling tours like the Tour of Valencia.

Q: Can I combine road cycling with wine tourism in Moixent?
A: Yes, the region around Moixent, particularly the Valley of Les Alcusses, is renowned for its thriving wine industry. You can easily combine your cycling adventures with visits to local wineries and rural farmhouses that produce high-quality Valencian wines, offering a unique cultural experience alongside your ride.

Q: Are there any routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Many routes in Moixent provide stunning panoramic views, especially those that ascend into the Serra Grossa mountain range. Climbs like &lt;strong&gt;Puerto del Moro&lt;/strong&gt; offer expansive vistas of the surrounding landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5285823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Panoramic view from La Plana&lt;/a&gt; is a specific highlight that offers breathtaking scenery.

Q: What is the longest road cycling route available in Moixent?
A: The longest road cycling route available is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38242612" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pou Clar Natural Area – Banyeres loop from Moixent&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 70.3 miles (113.2 km) and features a significant elevation gain of over 1,800 meters, making it a demanding ride for endurance cyclists.
tips:
The Pou Clar is a fluvial area of important natural and landscape value. This spot marks the beginning of the Clariano River, which in its first few meters has formed over time a series of pools on calcareous rock.
They can be enjoyed on hot days to take a refreshing swim, although they can be enjoyed throughout the year because there are very nice hiking trails there and it has picnic tables and parking for cars on the side of the road.
